mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior quarter, discount rates decline faster than earnings expectations adjust, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative after quarterly FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign of the signal is inverted in a rolling 2-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation rate that accelerates above recent trend forces the market to anticipate tighter policy and a higher discount rate, which lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above its 6-month moving average by 0.3pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation accelerations are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional average return is not lower than the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates worsening labor slack and weaker household income growth, which lowers near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns after 3-month unemployment increases are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations, or if the conditional return distribution is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS keeps rising while UNRATE stays low, payroll growth signals resilient income and consumption, supporting corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising PAYEMS with UNRATE below 4.5% does not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than the full sample, or if the interaction term is not stable across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS after a prior contraction reflects improving retail demand and revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth turns positive after at least 2 consecutive negative months
failure_condition: Reject if positive-turnaround RSAFS episodes fail to outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return distribution, or if the signal reverses sign in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO following prior strength signals tightening industrial conditions and weaker cyclical pricing power, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO dips below its 3-month average and SPX still posts positive 20-day returns in a majority of cases, or if the average conditional return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in HOUST and GDP growth captures broad demand acceleration, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-GDPC1 improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not positive in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, FEDFUNDS above neutral alongside elevated CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real rates and valuation compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 3% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if the tight-policy-and-high-inflation regime does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional mean return is not below zero across rolling 2-year windows.
